review_text: It is closed until September😡
There's a 'caravan' cafe out the front which serves the best coffee and Julie was up for a friendly chat.  It's little places like this that makes a trip memorable.

We stayed here recently and it was beautiful. The campsites and facilities were clean, and the staff went above and beyond in putting an afternoon together of animal feeding and chatting with everyone. Great entertainment for the kids and a lovely spot on the creek. The staff were lovely, I am not sure why there are negative comments about them. Thank you for having us and for a memorable experience.

Great View.
Amazing set up.
Spacious kitchen.
Poster looks. 👏👏

We arrived to the caravan park and were advised they had decided to no longer allow 4X4 truck LR to stay, due to previous incidents with the boom gate and cracked slabs. After asking i assume the owner , where would be a good place to turn around, I was promptly told that a b/double turns around at the check in area.. I advised that at no time would my set up be capable of such a thing, due to the draw bar and jack knifing issues.  I  have had 38 years experience in the earth moving and trucking industry.  I was able to  reverse into a position, directed by the owner, successfully, and left with out incident. This man may be a great farmer but maybe should just stick with farming. Steer away from this site if you have a larger set up.X4 truck LR to stay, due to previous incidents with the boom gate and cracked slabs. After asking i assume the owner , where would be a good place to turn around, I was promptly told that a b/double turns around at the check in area.. I advised that at no time would my set up be capable of such a thing, due to the draw bar and jack knifing issues.  I  have had 38 years experience in the earth moving and the  trucking industry.  I was able to  reverse into a position, advised by the owner, successfully, and left with out incident. This man may be a great farmer but maybe should just stick with farming. Steer away from this site if you have a larger set up. May I also say the park could be far more transparent on the web site with so called decisions 🤔 |Sincerely Keith

A family get away with 2 families with grandkids tenting and 2 camper vans - from checking in until check out the staff (owners) have been friendly, informative and extremely helpful. Thanks. |Great location, sites very large and not cramped. Lawns well watered and amenities block excellent. Relaxing beside the river with fire pits and BBQs.|At 4pm a great shop with Tassie devils and other animals 👍|Great coffee van on site every morning until 12mn and Huonville only a 15 minute will from the park. |Great weekend 👍👍 and we shall return |Many thanks to owners and staff

Very expensive camp site @ $70, expensive laundry @ $6 per load or dryer.  Rigidly enforced checkout times 10am but you can pay extra for a late checkout.  Nearest rubbish bin 250 metres. I guess they wanted to save on labour. No check in allowed before noon so you will be in a queue for a while if you arrive before 12.30pm.

First class caravan park, the owners constantly working to keep it in tip top condition.
Love the fact that hey have set tight rules around various things to ensure that everyone is happy.

Absolutely loved this place, extremely well run and very clean. Excellent value for money IMO. We had a tent site, thoughtfully allocated to us far away from the families (we have no kids). I can’t wait to come back!!

Great spot. We stayed here two nights and we particularly loved the farm show at 4pm each day. The grounds are lovely and the amenities block is very clean and well maintained. I'm not stoked about having to pay extra for a shower and think a timer would be sufficient but it seems to be the norm everywhere. Our son absolutely loved it here and the staff are wonderful and humorous.

I am appallued by the unkindness showen by the owner of your caravan park. She displayed extreamly rude behavoiurs towards me and my family, yelling infront of my two small children for no reason. This caraven park claims they are not open for booking which is ridiculus. Please do not go to this park, they do not deserve your money. #justice for rowan

I recently stopped by Huonville Caravan Park to have a look around as we were considering staying there, however I was very disappointed by the way we were treated by Rohan (owner?) He was rude & unwelcoming!

As someone simply looking for a place to stay I expected a basic level of courtesy & professionalism. I felt it was important to provide this feedback as customer service plays a big role in people choosing where to stay.

Very overpriced patch of nice lawn this place had nothing else to offer! Highly recommend going slightly further south to the choice of RV Camps that offer a much better experience, and at least have portable water on site.
Avoid this place!

Pretty poor customer service here.
There are better places to stay , look elsewhere.

Really big location lots of camper spaces and staff very nice.

Keep in mind they don’t do the devils feeding on Sundays, we only found out because we asked and unfortunately they won’t let you go back another day to see them even having paid for a night there. Also hot showers aren’t free despite being $50+ a night for a site

The amenities block is magnificent and the sites are well-spaced and excellently maintained. As they should be for the very high site fees. Given the latter, paying $1 for a shower is outrageous (also $6 each for washing machine and dryer).
I only found one bin to dispose of rubbish.
The worst feature however is the utter inflexibility of the check-out time of 10am. You can have a late check-out (for an additional $30 if you don't mind) or you can be charged an additional day's fee as a fine and be locked on site until the boom gates reopen at 12pm. Is this even legal??
Nicest thing about the place is the charming lady serving good coffee and delicious biscuits just outside the entry. Give her your patronage and find a friendlier campground.
Loved the river beach area and the Devil show. Facilities were excellent and very well kept
Lovely caravan park. Amazing showers. Was great place to refresh after a week of smaller donation campgrounds and convenient to everything in the area.

The staff at the reception was very rude. The lady was rude and unprofessional, as we were a big group, she time to time ask us to be quiet and not to disturb others and said she will ask us to leave just being a large group, although we were quite aware of the camp rules. the location was wonderful, close to all the amenities and yet peaceful and natural beauty. The river Infront of the campground was a bonus, you could swim or kayak or boat. The kitchen was good and everything else was good, other than the staff member.

The cleanest park we've visited. Spacious, level sites. Luxury amenities and plenty of them... each toilet in its own room, each shower in its own  room, too! Huge Camp Kitchen including BBQ, oven, hotplates, toasters, kettles, microwaves seating. Great laundry with multiple washers, dryers and clothes line in the sunshine. All spotless.|By the river, and an afternoon show of Tassie Devil feeding and intro to other farm animals.|Walk to Cafe, or a bit longer walk into Huonville town.|Awesome night sky as it is away from town lights.|Staff were friendly.
Fantastic facility , clean tidy beautifully maintaned , great service simpley the best , staff and owners are wonderful friendly highley recomend this magnifecent park .
I planned to stay here, but they want to surcharge me $20 for a late arrival. I've never encountered this before

One of the BEST van parks I've ever stayed in.  Views were spectacular, campsites were lush green, facilities were well thought out.  Picnic tables, BBQs and campfires scattered around the park for convenience and the amenities block is the designed how so many others should be. One big building with balconies all around.  Shower cubicles all down one side facing outward, toilets down the other side, laundry & basins around the back side.  The internal room being a large multi kitchenette room with tables, TV, multiple cooking stations.  The owners have put great thought into what campers need and some of the tent sites even have their own little undercover eating/storage area.  Thank you for thinking about what we the customers actually need
Absolutely loved our stay here so clean and tidy owners are super friendly would highly recommend 👌

Stayed 4 nights. A great caravan park, the grandkids loved it. Lots for them to do, fishing, swimming, platypus hunting, animal shows and of course the whole camping experience. Amenities spotless. A great camp kitchen available if needed. Highly recommend.
If you wanna start the camping, this is the best site for start. Lovely people, well organized and very very clean places. All customers know they have to clean after use. Very good place.
Okay place, although Rowan needs to improve customer service

These 2 crazy couple (owner) needs to learn more hospitality. ||We were invited to their farm show which started at 4pm unfortunately we were out and couldn’t get back to their show and they were very rude and were trying to get money in any excuse. ||Never and ever will go to these greedy and inhospitable camper. ||If you are with a family never book with them.

Excellent caravan park. Family friendly, quiet/not noisy. We had a powered site for our motorhome, clean new facilities. So much entertainment for the kids - Tassie Devils, Farm show, bubble show, large swing, lake to throw pebbles in & try spot platypuses, coffee/cafe van. Only con was that some showers didn’t work very well & can be costly with the constant $1 coin usage as a family of four. Not a biggie though! This is a great place to stay & explore everything Huon Valley & surrounds has to offer!

Luscious grass everywhere. Impressive kitchen and amenities. The daily animal show are entertaining and educational. The animals are all cared for lovingly. Friendly and knowledgeable staff. |The best caravan park we’ve been to.
Great park amazing amenities, Staff are great! Nice park

Great
GrandKids had the best time swimming in the river most of the day ‘everyday’ , watching the animal feeding and playing in the bubble machine bubbles
Rowen and Leanne best hosts
No need for mats as the grass is almost manicured
Seen the local platypus a couple of times
Spotless amenities and camp kitchen
2 minutes from town yet feel secluded

We have stayed in the on-site vans for a couple of nights for the past 2 years. A little pricey but had friends staying there so convenient to get the van. The van had a washing machine but no clothes line or washing powder but could buy washing powder for $1 at reception. Given we had paid $200 you would think they would chuck in some washing powder. I suppose if we wanted to hang out washing out we could peg it out near the ammenities and have our washing smell like sewage. A small fold out clothes line would not be that expensive. Our toilet in the van had not been emptied and was nearly full. We were talking about it at our friends site and next thing old mate goes zooming past on his 4 wheeler and changed it late at night. Was it a coincidence that we just talked about it or was he listening in on our conversation. The public toilets last year were very clean. I found them to be unclean every time I used them and some of the time no toilet paper. Our friends had a visitor come see them and had to pay $30 to visit for a couple of hours. Then she was told by the unfriendly female owner that she drove too fast going in but the weird thing was she walked in? The owners are unfriendly and need to lighten up a bit. Sorry that is not true all the time, they are really friendly when taking your money for over priced accommodation. The whole site is great and the grounds are very well maintained but the owners need to be more hospitable.

Second visit here. Our favourite camp site in the area. The facilities are the best. $1 for 4 min shower. Lovely individual shower rooms. The owners are great. We opted for a premium river pitch on both visits. Say hi to Sassy the sheep. There is a farm show at 4pm and they also have Tassie Devils. Close to town for all the amenities. DS cafe is the best for brekkie.

We drove by and decided to pull in here and this was hands down was the best caravan park we’ve been to. The Tassie devil feeding was a great bonus. The ammenities and surrounding location in the park was a 10/10. All the staff there were super friendly, Would highly recommend stopping by and supporting great people.

Décevant... à l'arrivée pas le temps de se présenter et de dire quoi que ce soit que l'on me met la feuille à remplir sous le nez sans me laisser le temps de parler...
50 dollars la nuit pour un emplacement sans électricité et il faut payer les douches ! 1$ les 4 mn... Pas de wifi... Le camping était vide et on est venu nous chasser a 10h tapante... dommage car l endroit est bien, les sanitaires sont propres. Mais je ne comprends pas pourquoi ils enferment des diables de tasmanie dans des petites cages...

Wish i could take this park everywhere with us.
Amazing place and working farm with two senior tassie devils and other animals and Rowans humour thrown in lol.
Beautiful very modern facilities set in a lovely park its a credit to the owners they have made a perfect caravan park they also have tassie devil feeding time 😀

It was really nice to be greeted by someone in the office. Very clean and environmentally friendly park with wonderful staff always happy to help and have a chat. Amenities very clean and accessible. Well done Rowan and Leanne for running a happy and safe environment for humans and our fur friends.
Great place.  Great people.  Go and stay a night or two.  Can't go wrong.

Lovely area ... Close to shops and boat ride
Bit expensive when extras on same plot in a swag or tent was $30 each ...and the cost should include showers .$1 for 4 mins .... $65 a night ...and $6 each for washer or dryer ... So many rules .. one car not two ..  dog friendly but lots were barking . And absolutely no visitors allowed !!!
Beautiful fire pits with large tables .
Lovely clean amenities .and commune area

A $1 for 4 minute showers I just thought that was a bit rude
$6 for washing machines and dryers
In all the other caravan parks I was only paying $4
And didn’t have to pay for my showers
It was a very clean park I’ll give them that
Fees start at $55 for a site
Lovely place, clean toilets BUT, costs a fortune compared to a lot of other places and then found the showers are coin operated. $1 for 4 minutes and it’s cold at the start so you can’t get in.

We stopped here for one night and I recommend upgrading to a lake view site. The amenities are brand new and super clean and a bonus is watching the animal experience including seeing Tassie devils being fed. The staff are very friendly and accommodating, highly recommend.
Don't need to book,  right on the river,  great location for day trips.

Fantastic location and the caravan sights are huge with lovely soft grass. Facilities are very clean. Treated river water is at each sigh it has a bit of tannin in it so it’s recommended to boil before drinking. The farm show every day is great for the kids as is the devil feeding.

The facilities in my opinion were top class for a caravan park. Spoiled us for any other facilities after that. The toilets and showers were all unisex with a common area for the sinks. Very good showers. Really nice laundry and the camp kitchen was lovely.

This caravan park is an environmentally friendly park. It has a Tasmanian Devil feeding show, free range chocks and orchard and it’s on a river. The camp kitchen and the toilets and showers are first class
Great spot for the family, silly to have to purchase a second site for a second car. Wouldn't even let us drive in to unload then remove second car from site
A great park with great amenities... but the park rules are oppressive.
I honestly think this is the best caravan park I have ever stayed at. I thoroughly recommend it! The free Tasmania Devil show just tops it off.

We loved our 3 nights here. The managers are passionate about providing a top class camping experience. We received a warm welcome and allocated a site out of wind and close to bathrooms. The site was so pleasant - soft green grass. A few comfortable bare foot days and no dirt in the camper trailer - such a luxury. The camp kitchen is one of the best we’ve seen in 5 months travelling. The bathrooms and laundry were pristine and cleverly laid out with plenty to go around. And there was a separate urinal room for men’s convenience and reduced wet seat comfort. I was put off at first by the reviews commenting on the rules. But they were nothing that other caravan parks don’t give out. I highly recommend this park.
Very clean and tidy.

What a fantastic caravan park.  All facilities are spotless, the owners are very accommodating in selecting a comfortable site.

I would highly recommend this park and would definitely return.

highly recommend this caravan park to everybody so relaxing and great to have grass under feet instead of dust the animal show at 4pm every day is so good and you can join in feeding them what a great experience
very clean, very good location, friendly owner, maybe too expensive for backpackers

Great weekend stay loved it great amenities, super friendly people , only negotiable is guy who sits in office,all day winging about speed limits 10 kph well n good it's about safety to all uses but he gets around on big ass ride on mower all day n night traveling on the same roads we do clearly doing way more than 10 ks per hour were is his duty off care to all uses , it's hard to stick exactly on 10 ks we were barley doing over 10 ks but he is so anal about speed when he doesn't stick to speed limit him self great weekend Mr handle bar mostash but be real and abide by ruel ya self all in all five star stay

Very tidy , clean, family friendly caravan park.We enjoyed being close to a Huonville facilities and all that has to offer. We did however found it odd that there are no bins through out  the park or any recycling facilities. The sites are very open without  any privacy and with the park manger Rowan patrolling the park on his buggy constantly and the 2000 cameras he has set up, it is a touch weird. Put on top of that we got charged close to 300 for 3 nights (2 adults , 4 kids) and still had to pay for showers, I would probably head 20 mins up the road to a more relaxed, chilled and more scenic camp site.  Ps the kids did love the animals and the coffee shop owner is a lovely lady
Wonderful place to stay very tidy and clean. Great toilets and showers not to mention the Tasmanian devil feeding each day at 4pm.Its definitely one of my favourite parks to stay in tasmania.
Lovely grassed sites. Plenty of room between sites, not squashed in at all. Clean modern camp kitchen, toilets, amenities, laundry.

Lovely well maintained park in a great spot to explore the area!
On arrival we were very impressed with the way that this park looked. Amenities are lovely although could do with cleaning a little more regularly especially during busy times.
We were a large group of friends, mostly all in our 40’s/50’s  with the exception of a couple in our group having the pleasure of their adult children with them. However if you stay at this park expect RULES! We have never stayed anywhere in Australia (we have traveled extensively) with so many rules and a ruler that seems to think he is a higher authority. I’m not sure if the manager is the owner? Is new to the business? Or should be retiring? But if he continues the way he’s going from our experiences, this park will suffer. Oh and two cameras on the dump point?  Not to mention if you happen to be late checking out expect a fine, yes, even though you are leaving a site that you have shared (ie no one coming into that same suit as your parents are still there….you cop a $50 fine!!?? This happened to our friends
You pay premium price for a site, then need to pay for the use of showers!?
Yes, caravan parks have and need rules, but this park takes all of that to the next level. Do yourself a favour and find an alternative spot to stay if you’re after a relaxing break away. We do not recommend this park.
Our saving grace was the lovely group we were traveling with which went on to greatly enjoy the other planned parks that we stayed in.
Terrible pricing and expensive, rules for everything, and u have to pay to have a shower on top of that

Amazing park for families. The grass is so luscious and beautiful. A little bit expensive but the free animal show is pretty cool for the little ones. Showers are $1 for 4 minutes, I think for the price of sites $55-$75 they could cut the shower charge out. River is good for a refreshing dip and the off lead dog area at the front of the park is a greato option for dog owners. Great park all in all, definitely recommend.
What a gem. Just beautiful. Spacious sites, all green lawn, amenities are amazing. Would highly recommend this spot.
Beautiful location and excellent facilities. Congratulations on all your hard work to make this a premier attraction in the Huon Valley.

Pros: Lovely green caravan site with power and water. Facilities are well maintained and clean. It is nice to have a shower in a separate cubicle, animal show was very interesting, early (10.15am) checkin appreciated so we could begin sightseeing the area.
Cons: there are no rubbish bins in the park except for 2x 60litre ones in the camp kitchen and the skip bin at the entry to this huge 200 site park, we had to park our caravan a long way from the amenities because the close ones needed to be mowed, layout of amenities in a square meant that toilets were on one side of the building, camp kitchen in the middle with showers on the opposite side and hand basins along the third wall, expensive @ $55pn for 2 people or $65 for river views plus $1 for a shower (joke), no instructions on how to operate the hot water for the showers, $1machine did not work twice for hubby so after spending $3 for 2 cold showers he complained to Rowen who told him he must have had the handle in the wrong position which is rubbish as it worked for me with the handle in the on position for hot water. Rowen needs to improve his customer service.

Die Lage ist wunderschön und ideal als Ausgangspunkt wenn man die Gegend südlich von Hobart erkunden möchte. Allerdings ist der Platz mit 75$ mit großem Abstand der Teuerste den wir im Laufe unserer Reise hatten, was auch nicht durch eine besonders gute Ausstattung zu erklären ist (gibt wohl auch günstigere Stellplätze, das wurde uns aber verschwiegen). Kein WiFi, duschen kostet extra. Außerdem ist das Schreiben mit den Infos nicht so richtig nett - s. Foto.
Schade drum...
Really nice caravan park but expensive! Expect to pay $55-$65 for a site and then you have to pay for a shower on top, what a joke!

Was a very clean and open caravan park. Nice bbq facilities and very nice amenities.  Would be very nice in summer next to the river.
But seriously we played $65 for a night here and still has to find the $1 coins for the hot water shower? Unaustralian as charging for sauce with a meat pie.

What an amazing park. Right from the start perfect service and even better once we entered the park. Pristine camp grounds and first class amenities. Loved the river side spot watching the sun going down , what a great way to end the day. Will definitely be back x

First stop for our 4 week motorhome journey through Tasmania. Very well presented property, with well sized sites, clean and accessible amenities, plenty of wildlife around, and a great range of park facilities.

Thanks for having us!

Beautiful caravan park with lovely clean amenities and friendly people. The Devil feeding and milking demo was a lovely surprise, but I think Sassy the sheep stole the whole show. Would stay here again in a heartbeat.

We have just spent 3 nights at this wonderful park, with the largest, greenest and most well manicured site we've ever stayed at. We had a double slab site, with a view looking straight up the river. We went to the 4pm feeding of the Devils, and the milking of a very "contented" looking cow. Spotlessly clean amenities.
Well done Rowen and Leanne.

This place is amazing for children and the whole family, the campsites are very generous and the camp kitchen and shower facilities are next level amazing. They do a daily show at 4pm which includes feeding the tassie devils and varies from sheering a sheep, sheepdog show, milking a cow and the massive bubble machine, with all shows they hand out baby ducks and chicken for everyone to have a hold of, great for kayaking and they have made a beach by the river for the kids 😀 the river is very slow running and has a shallow end and a deep end, simply amazing I would highly recommend 😀 👍

Disappointing noisy with kids running around screaming and dogs barking, adults drinking with loud voices and noise of the generator early morning and afternoon.  It's not worth for the money we pay in unpowered site.  It's like a refugees camp, no peace to unwind. I've been to a very basic caravan park but very relaxing and quiet, and not packed like sardines.

Perfect location for exploring the Huon valley and beyond. Only 4 stars as not all cookers working in camp kitchen…. And there were a lot of us trying to cook!
Great showers and toilets. Uni sex. Only 4 washbasins for everyone though.
Summer bakery up the road, the BEST pastries. And DCs Cafe for coffee and 1950’s charm…

Great RV and caravan park. Awesome setup and very well maintained and kept. Fantastic amenities also very clean. Beautiful flat grassy sites. I would highly recommend only at $50 a night is a bit steep for unpowered and to still have to pay for showers was a bit much, but still I would stay again.

Amenities were spotless and abundant, our camp-site was by the river and very peaceful and quiet. Park has sites that cater for everyone. The sites are very large and very well maintained. Also the sounds of Tassie devils at night while having a whiskey
Mike and Kelly

This park is well kept and the amenities and camp kitchen are well designed to cater for large numbers. However, you do pay a premium price to use this park and are then hit up for more $ to get hot water for your shower (which was not very clean) and the laundry is more expensive than any other one we have encountered. To top it off, we arrived on a warm day and specifically asked for a shady site. Although there were no other campers within several hundred metres, we were given a particular campsite to use that had no shade at all. There was no freedom to go and choose from any unpowered camp site. There were some options given from cameras at the office, but it was difficult to ascertain just what the site truly looked like from that angle. Would not recommend this caravan park.
Very clean, sites quite large, the beach at the river is fun. The tassie devil display is very nice and extra activities as well. Camp kitchen for those doing tent camping is great
well maintained Caravan park.

Nice park. Lots of rules and the owner driver's around inforcing them, hard to relax. Very expensive, we paid extra for the premium river spot with no view of the river. $50 deposits for each dog that is not listed on the website, and we have 2 small dogs. $25 for an extra adult on top of the $60. 2 nights with 2 extra adults ( that did not end up coming) plus dog deposit would have cost $320 up front. Strictly no guests allowed. 10 am sharp checkout or pay for another night.

We camped in tent in a premium river spot during the summer school holidays. It was $70 for the 3 (2adults one child) of us plus $1/4mins for hot showers. We booked for 4 days but stayed only 3.

Our child had a wonderful camping adventure. Swimming in the river, foam parties, animal shows and looking after baby ducks being some of the highlights.

Our  experience wasn’t horrible but wasn’t as much fun. From a practical perspective the park was lacking for us.
There is little to no shade. Sitting and supervising a child in the river was hot with no escape from the sun. Our stay was also windy so we were unable to put up temporary shade and there were no permanent shade fixtures or trees to sit under. The sand from the make shift beach blowing everywhere added to the experience.

Other parents often didn’t supervise their children in the water sometime we were the only adults at the river with 6-10 kids and several of them being under 5. We had to rescue one child who couldn’t swim and was caught on a log in the water.
The river is the main feature of the park, it’s where the children gather to play.
There is one wooden swing that needs WD40. It squeaked very loudly and was an unpleasant sound from 630am-1030pm. It was in front of our site.

The one car only policy meant that we were unable to make an adequate wind break around the tent  and  going somewhere meant lugging day trip items out of the park to use the second car as it wasn’t able to be driven into the park to be loaded or unloaded.

Our camp neighbours stayed up talking and drinking until 1 am  and while they weren’t rowdy they were loud enough to hear each word clearly without trying. That combined with  kids using the swing from 6:30am-10:30pm meant for very long days.

Ease of use is not a feature of the park and we have stayed other places that have been far more practical, affordable and relaxing as parents on a family trip. It is a beautiful spot with clean facilities and some different experiences for kids to do.
Wonderful park, attention to detail was spot on. We used the location as a base to go up to Hobart, Port arthur,  down south to Cockle Beach.

Great location. Stayed for 1 night before traveling down to the Hastings cave. Very friendly staff. Great little farm display. Very clean ammenities block. Great camp kitchen. Platypus in the river is a real treat. A play ground or something for the kids to do would have been icing on the cake.

Paid $60 for a riverside powered site, the most I’ve ever paid! This is supposed to cover power and water hookup, only they don’t tell you until you get your site details and have paid that the water isn’t drinkable! On top of this $1 per 4min shower which is cold for the first minute! The excuse given for this was that Tassie used twice the national average in water, hardly an argument when you charge what you do for a patch of grass and some power! And $6 per load of laundry, that’s over the top! And people seem to be raving about the camp kitchen, I have seen far better in parks that charged half the amount! Having travelled through SA, Vic and now Tassie, this has to be the most expensive park with the least amenities available. I would highly suggest to the owners to lower their fees or up their game with amenities, and provide drinkable water, because this is Australia, not a third world country!

We did two nights here, was a bit anxious before going due to the bad reviews on here, but the gentleman was very pleasant to us, excellent with the kids that were at the park, had them screaming and enjoying themselves during the bubbles he distributed on the lawn.
Maybe he doesn’t like adults?? We will use this park again. Thank you

Huon valley is a beautiful place, easily placed for access to Hobart etc.  The caravan park is a short stroll away from Huonville for shops and other services.  There appears to be a flat rate charge for sites at the park regardless of powered and not powered.  The park also has a charge for showers (4min @ 1$). That may put some people off, however this caravan park gives you large, clean camping sites. For non powered some sites have small storage shelters, but all sites have either a slab or thick grass.  The camp kitchen is large and well equipped. There are around 10 or 11 separate clean showers and toilets.  We never had to queue or wait for a shower.  The camp kitchen has USB wall chargers installed and available. Along the edge of the park is a river where you can see platypus, ducks and the occasional trout. The park also has cows and sheep which gives the impression of a work farm stay. There are also Tasmanian Devils, with a daily feed time. We saw three devils across various feedings. We enjoyed our stay at the park, it was clean and spacious.

Very peaceful location for families with caravans and campers with modern amenities- camp kitchen, laundry, showers, toilets. Also Tassie devils which you can watch being fed at 4pm daily. If needing supplies it's a short drive into Huonville. Remember  that for all these facilities it comes at a cost - $50 a night for campers which may be expensive for some. Use it well and it's a bonus.

The old shrivelled up man that owns the place needs to learn basic human decency. He blew up over a small misunderstanding, accused us of things that did not happen and refused to calm down. When he came over to us for a second time he refused to apologise for his disgusting immature behaviour and instead told us that we shouldn’t write a complaint. Terrible service. Highly recommend never staying here!!!
Great park … highly recommend

Excellent place to stay. The feeding of the Devils carried out each afternoon at 4.00. Great to see and even greater the information given on our fierce but cute little endangered marsupials. A family run park that is very comfortable and relaxing.  Also has platypus in Huon River that runs through the Park.

Amazing beautiful place.
This park gas it all.
Great amenities
Great owners
Great location
So freindly and quite
Highly recommend

Huge sites and incredibly well maintained great amenities on the river lots of wildlife lovely hosts, probably the best caravan park we have ever stayed in,felt like home away from home highly recommend
Had the most amazing time. Loved it so much we cancelled our last night in hobart to stay there again.
Excellent caravan park, well kept  spacious sites. Amenities are many and exceptionally maintained. Great Vale as a base to visit Huon Valley  Bruny Island and Hobart.

Only park I’ve been where several resident platypus families live. We saw several which is amazing. Big sites in pristine park lots of birds open space scenery is just lovely in the park itself. Creek runs through park so can fish as well Huon river just 100 metres away. Amenities modern clean and well laid out. Very big park 26 acres so check site distance to amenities as can be a decent walk. Owners take pride in their park and it shows.
Lovley peaceful place to stay great park

One of the best, this Caravan Park
Peaceful, space for all, beautifully manicured grounds, very family friendly.
Totally recommend it 100%
Jenny Raczka

Amazing peaceful location with well cared for, modern amenities- camp kitchen, laundry, showers, toilets. Beautiful manicured spaces surrounded by a pretty (large) stream with platypuses. Also rescued Tassie devils which you can watch being fed - informative and interesting. A very short drive or wander into Huonville for all supplies and cafes. Thoroughly recommend Huon Valley Caravan Park, well done Rowan & Leanne for creating a wonderful place, so glad we stayed 😊

We stayed here for 5 nights and this has been the highlight of our trip so far. We booked here for the Tassie devil daily feeding and it was amazing to see them so up close. The park was very lush and green and all amenities were always clean no matter what time you went up to them. We stayed on a water front site and it was beautiful.
